title: Morning Session
section: A fast first win changes the room
text:
A fast first win changes the room




Open with momentum. The point of the morning is not depth. It is confidence. Tell the room they only need one working flow before lunch.

title: Morning Session
section: Sign-in should feel easy
text:
Sign-in should feel easy























Access workspace







Open Copilot Studio







Find create and test areas







Start building
















Presenter cue


Name only four surfaces: create, instruct, ground, test.




Do not narrate every menu. Keep the interface tour anchored in user intent so beginners do not feel lost.

title: Morning Session
section: The interface should feel familiar fast
text:
The interface should feel familiar fast





Look for

create
instructions
knowledge
test chat




If you have a real screenshot, replace the placeholder and point visually rather than verbally.

title: Morning Session
section: A first agent needs only role and boundaries
text:
A first agent needs only role and boundaries






Minimal recipe


Role. Goals. Boundaries.



You are a helpful assistant for public-facing utility questions.

Goals:
- explain clearly
- stay grounded

Boundaries:
- do not invent details
- say when information is missing

Say out loud that this is intentionally short. The teaching point is that behavior can move before any extra data or tools are added.

title: Morning Session
section: Test prompts should reveal the edges
text:
Test prompts should reveal the edges



Good question
Clear and answerable



Ambiguous question
Forces a follow-up



Impossible question
Forces uncertainty handling



This is where attendees usually understand why boundaries matter. Make them test all three question types.

title: Morning Session
section: Good files beat clever prompts
text:
Good files beat clever prompts


Transition from instructions to grounding by saying that the fastest path to usefulness is often a clean document, not a more elaborate prompt.

title: Morning Session
section: Choice creates energy in the lab
text:
Choice creates energy in the lab



Zones brief
Word document



Solar capacity
Spreadsheet



Price history
Spreadsheet or CSV



Keep the class aligned by giving structured choice rather than open-ended choice. Variety helps without creating chaos.

title: Morning Session
section: Grounding works when the source is clean
text:
Grounding works when the source is clean























Clean source







Better retrieval







Better answers







More trust
















Teaching line


When the answers are weak, inspect the source before rewriting the prompt.




This is one of the most useful habits to teach. It reframes troubleshooting around source quality, not just prompt wording.

title: Morning Session
section: Website knowledge makes the pattern feel real
text:
Website knowledge makes the pattern feel real





Pick pages that are

factual
stable
easy to summarize




Use a boring but factual page. Avoid marketing-heavy pages because they distract from the grounding lesson.

title: Morning Session
section: MCP should feel useful before it feels technical
text:
MCP should feel useful before it feels technical



























Client







Server







Tools







Resources







Prompts
















Morning message


MCP is how agents gain structured access to outside capabilities.




Do not teach implementation here. This is just a bridge to the afternoon build track.

title: Morning Session
section: Publishing turns a private demo into a shared tool
text:
Publishing turns a private demo into a shared tool



























Build







Test







Review







Choose channel







Publish











Frame publishing as ownership and governance, not just as a final button. That lands better with mixed audiences.

title: Morning Session
section: By lunch everyone should feel capable
text:
By lunch everyone should feel capable



One agent
Working and testable



One grounding path
File or website



One clear next step
Afternoon build or applied use case



Close the morning with reassurance. The afternoon can go deeper, but the morning should already feel successful on its own.

title: Resources
text:
This section points attendees and instructors to the reusable assets behind the course.



Curated notes on websites, Revealjs, code highlighting, and GitHub Pages live in .github/quarto-docs/.



The exercise datasets live under data/ and attendee-assets/knowledge-sources/.



Python MCP examples and templates live under mcp-servers/.

title: Start fast and reduce friction
text:
By the end of this step, every attendee should be signed in, oriented, and ready to click through the first build flow without hesitation.


10 min

Target duration for access, orientation, and reassurance.



1 goal

Remove uncertainty before introducing agent design.









ImportantInstructor move



Narrate the interface in terms of intent: where to create, where to ground, where to test, and where to publish.





























Access workspace







Open Copilot Studio







Scan the left navigation







Find agent settings







Find test chat and publish paths

title: Start fast and reduce friction
section: What to point out live
text:
What to point out live

where a new agent starts
where instructions live
where knowledge sources are attached
where testing happens before publishing







TipKeep the framing light



Attendees do not need a taxonomy lecture here. They need confidence that the product is navigable.

title: MCP explains how agents do more
text:
Attendees leave the morning knowing what MCP is for, without needing implementation details yet.






























Client







Server







Tools







Resources







Prompts













The environment where the user interacts.



The external capability provider.



A callable function with structured inputs.








TipMorning message



MCP is useful because it gives agents a standard way to access tools and data. The afternoon shows how to build one.
crumbs: MCP explains how agents do more

title: Instructions create the first win
text:
Attendees create a first agent using instructions only, so they can feel the behavior shift before any external data is added.



A tight role statement beats a broad ambition statement.



Tell the agent what not to invent and how to admit uncertainty.



Summaries, bullet points, and short follow-up questions create better first demos.








NoteWorking pattern



Start with role, goals, and boundaries. Then test with three realistic prompts before refining anything else.





























Role







Goals







Boundaries







Test prompts







Refine tone and output shape

title: Instructions create the first win
section: Suggested first structure
text:
Suggested first structure
You are a helpful assistant for public-facing utility questions.

Goals:
- explain clearly
- stay grounded in supplied knowledge
- keep answers concise first

Boundaries:
- do not invent policies or prices
- say when information is missing
- ask a clarifying question when needed






TipFacilitation tip



Have attendees test one good question, one ambiguous question, and one impossible question.

text: This section is the attendee-facing shortcut library. The raw source files live in attendee-assets/ so the website can stay aligned with the lab files.

title: Copy and Paste
section: Authoring Rule
text:
Authoring Rule
Whenever we add a new snippet for class, we should add it to attendee-assets/ first and then surface it here.

title: Course Overview
text:
The day is designed for a mixed audience:

all attendees in the morning
advanced users in the afternoon

title: Course Overview
text:
The day is designed for a mixed audience:

all attendees in the morning
advanced users in the afternoon

title: Course Overview
section: Session Map
text:
Session Map








Step
Focus
Intended outcome




1
Sign in and orient
Everyone can access the interface and understand the main surfaces


2
First agent
Everyone creates a working agent with instructions only


3
Files as knowledge
Everyone connects a document or spreadsheet


4
Web knowledge
Everyone configures a website-backed knowledge source


5
Available MCP servers
Everyone understands what MCP adds to agents


6
Publishing channels
Everyone sees how agents can be shared


7
Dev environment
Advanced users install VS Code, uv, and Copilot


8
Build an MCP server
Advanced users expose two tools from Python


9
Deploy on Azure
Advanced users understand the deployment shape


10
Connect to Copilot Studio
Advanced users attach the server as a remote capability


11
Brainstorming
Teams sketch their own internal use cases

title: Course Overview
section: Variation Strategy
text:
Variation Strategy
We want variety in selected moments rather than forcing every attendee through the exact same assets.


File grounding
Attendees can choose between a Word brief, a spreadsheet about solar capacity, or a time-series pricing file.


Website grounding
Attendees can test a utility-style public website, a policy-style page, or a facts-and-FAQ page.


MCP examples
The afternoon can use one canonical walkthrough while still offering alternative template servers afterwards.








NotePublic repo rule



The site and sample content should stay generic and reusable. The real client context informs the examples, but it should not appear by name in the repo.

title: Afternoon Track
text:
Show advanced users how to build, inspect, deploy, and connect their own MCP server.



Standardize the editor, Python tooling, and command rhythm.



Explain only the architecture needed to make the build legible.



Turn plain Python functions into MCP tools with clear structure.



Walk from local server to remote endpoint to Copilot Studio connection.



End with realistic team use cases rather than abstract ideas.

title: Afternoon Track
text:
Show advanced users how to build, inspect, deploy, and connect their own MCP server.



Standardize the editor, Python tooling, and command rhythm.



Explain only the architecture needed to make the build legible.



Turn plain Python functions into MCP tools with clear structure.



Walk from local server to remote endpoint to Copilot Studio connection.



End with realistic team use cases rather than abstract ideas.

title: Afternoon Track
section: Emphasis
text:
Emphasis
The walkthrough should make the code transparent, not magical. The afternoon slide deck will therefore include line-by-line commentary, progressive code highlighting, and architecture diagrams.






TipCanonical stack



The baseline implementation uses the official MCP Python SDK with the FastMCP authoring pattern and a deployment shape suited for HTTP-based hosting.








TipTeaching rhythm



Keep the afternoon code-first, but pause at each architectural jump so the room does not lose the plot.

title: Deployment should feel like the next step
text:
Participants understand the shape of moving from a local server to a hosted endpoint that Copilot Studio can connect to.






























Local project







Container image







Container Apps







Remote endpoint







Copilot Studio













If the server is unclear locally, hosting will not fix it.



Package the working server, not the half-working one.



Only connect after the endpoint contract is understood.








ImportantKeep the story linear



The room should understand why each deployment step exists before the Azure details show up.
crumbs: Deployment should feel like the next step

title: Brainstorming works best after a concrete demo
text:
Participants leave with a realistic idea of what they could automate or expose through a future MCP server.



Which repeated search task could become a tool.



Which outside API or data feed could enrich answers.



Which tasks require approval, logging, or human review.





























Find a pain point







Name the user







Name the needed data







Name the needed tool







Name the risk or approval rule
















TipWorkshop prompt



If you had one safe tool to expose next month, what would it do and who would trust it enough to use it?
crumbs: Brainstorming works best after a concrete demo

title: About
text:
This site is the source material for a reusable Copilot Studio training day.
It combines:

Quarto website pages
Revealjs presentations
attendee-ready copy and paste snippets
starter MCP server projects in Python
deployment notes for Azure Container Apps

The examples are intentionally practical, public, and easy to adapt for future deliveries.



 Back to top

title: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern
text:
Advanced users see how plain Python functions become discoverable MCP tools.



























Python function







Type hints







Docstring







FastMCP decorator







Inspectable tool
















ImportantCore lesson



The important transformation is not complexity. It is structure.
crumbs: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern

crumbs: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern

title: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern
section: Minimal example
text:
Minimal example
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

mcp = FastMCP("demo")

@mcp.tool()
def ping(name: str) -> str:
    """Return a simple greeting."""
    return f"Hello, {name}!"
crumbs: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern

title: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern
section: Canonical walkthrough shape
text:
Canonical walkthrough shape

one lookup-style tool
one data or status tool
short descriptions with good parameter names
a run path that works locally before deployment







NoteTeaching prompt



Ask the room which parts are business logic and which parts are protocol glue.
crumbs: Two small tools are enough to teach the pattern

title: A smooth environment keeps the class moving
text:
Advanced users leave setup mode quickly and get to runnable Python with minimal friction.



Use one editor for Quarto, Python, terminal work, and Copilot support.



Create environments and run projects with short, repeatable commands.



Use it as a coding assistant, not as a replacement for understanding the build.

























Install tools







Create project







Sync dependencies







Run sample server
















NoteBaseline commands



Use uv lock, uv sync, and uv run as the repeated rhythm for the afternoon.
crumbs: A smooth environment keeps the class moving

title: MCP concepts should feel concrete
text:
Participants understand the small number of protocol concepts that matter for building a first server.


































Client







MCP server







Tool







Resource







Prompt







Transport













A function the client can call.



Data the client can retrieve.



Reusable guidance that shapes a task.








TipTeaching boundary



Concentrate on tools first. They produce the fastest visible value during a live class.

title: Copilot Studio Day Course
text:
One-day format
This repository packages the material for a one-day Copilot Studio course with two distinct modes:

a practical morning for all attendees
a deeper afternoon for super users who want to build and deploy MCP servers

The public repo stays anonymous. The examples are framed around a Swedish municipal utility scenario without naming the client.

title: Copilot Studio Day Course
section: What This Repository Will Hold
text:
What This Repository Will Hold


Morning enablement
Guided steps for signing in, building a first agent, grounding with files and websites, reviewing existing MCP connections, and publishing to channels.


Afternoon build track
A code-first walkthrough using uv, Python, FastMCP, inspector tooling, and Azure Container Apps deployment.


Reusable class assets
Copy-paste instructions, public datasets, template MCP servers, and future video scripts that attendees can revisit after class.

title: Copilot Studio Day Course
section: Course Flow
text:
Course Flow

Morning fundamentals in Copilot Studio
Lunch break and environment setup
Afternoon coding and deployment walkthrough
Closing brainstorming session for attendee scenarios

title: Copilot Studio Day Course
section: Jump In
text:
Jump In

Start with Course Overview
Open the Morning Track
Open the Afternoon Track
Use Copy and Paste for ready-made snippets
Present from Morning Slides or Afternoon Slides

title: Good files beat clever prompts
text:
Attendees see that a well-chosen file instantly makes the agent more useful and more trustworthy.



Word document about Swedish electricity zones and municipalities.



Spreadsheet with installed solar capacity.



Spreadsheet with electricity prices over time.











ImportantWhat we are teaching



The file is not just content. It is the shape of the answers the agent can produce.

























Clean file







Better grounding







Better retrieval







Better answers

title: Good files beat clever prompts
text:
Attendees see that a well-chosen file instantly makes the agent more useful and more trustworthy.



Word document about Swedish electricity zones and municipalities.



Spreadsheet with installed solar capacity.



Spreadsheet with electricity prices over time.











ImportantWhat we are teaching



The file is not just content. It is the shape of the answers the agent can produce.

























Clean file







Better grounding







Better retrieval







Better answers

title: Good files beat clever prompts
section: Simple evaluation questions
text:
Simple evaluation questions

Can the agent find the right fact quickly
Can the agent summarize a table without guessing
Can the agent say when the file does not contain the answer







TipInstructor shortcut



If a file is messy, tell attendees that this is a data-quality lesson rather than a prompt-quality lesson.

title: Morning Track
text:
Give every attendee enough confidence to create, ground, and publish an agent in Copilot Studio.



Get everyone logged in and oriented without turning the opening into a lecture.



Show how role, goals, and boundaries shape the first agent.



Use documents and spreadsheets to show visible grounding value.



Turn a public page into a knowledge source and test it live.



Introduce the concept without drowning the room in protocol details.



Explain where agents go after the build phase.

title: Morning Track
text:
Give every attendee enough confidence to create, ground, and publish an agent in Copilot Studio.



Get everyone logged in and oriented without turning the opening into a lecture.



Show how role, goals, and boundaries shape the first agent.



Use documents and spreadsheets to show visible grounding value.



Turn a public page into a knowledge source and test it live.



Introduce the concept without drowning the room in protocol details.



Explain where agents go after the build phase.

title: Morning Track
section: Attendee Options
text:
Attendee Options


Option A
Work with a document about Swedish electricity zones.


Option B
Work with a spreadsheet on installed solar capacity.


Option C
Work with a pricing dataset over time.

title: Morning Track
section: Outputs
text:
Outputs
By lunch, each attendee should have:

one working agent
one grounded data source
one web-based knowledge configuration
one publishing target they understand







TipTeaching rhythm



Keep the morning hands-on, forgiving, and fast. The purpose is momentum.

title: Publishing turns a demo into a product
text:
Attendees understand the difference between building an agent for themselves and publishing an agent for others.



A natural surface for collaboration and quick discovery.



Useful when the audience already works inside the broader suite.



Important before any wider rollout.





























Build agent







Test agent







Review permissions







Choose channel







Publish with owner
















ImportantFacilitation tip



Frame publishing as governance, ownership, and discoverability, not just as a button at the end.
crumbs: Publishing turns a demo into a product

title: Publishing turns a demo into a product
section: Before lunch, attendees should know
text:
Before lunch, attendees should know

who the agent is for
which channel makes sense
who would own future updates
crumbs: Publishing turns a demo into a product

title: Web knowledge scales the pattern
text:
Attendees understand why a web source becomes a durable knowledge base when a single file is too static or too narrow.




the information already lives on the web
updates happen outside the class repo
attendees need a realistic grounding example





navigation clutter
content that is too promotional
pages with weak factual structure


























Public page







Index content







Create knowledge source







Test grounded questions
















NoteDesign principle



Pick pages with stable factual content, not flashy landing pages. The cleaner the page, the easier it is to explain the result.

title: Web knowledge scales the pattern
section: Good demo prompts
text:
Good demo prompts

What does this page say about the service scope
Summarize the key facts in five bullets
What information is still missing for a customer decision

title: Afternoon Session
section: The afternoon should make MCP feel buildable
text:
The afternoon should make MCP feel buildable




Set the expectation: this is not protocol theory. This is a build story from Python function to usable endpoint.

title: Afternoon Session
section: One architecture picture beats a long definition
text:
One architecture picture beats a long definition























Copilot Studio







Remote MCP endpoint







FastMCP server







Public APIs











Walk left to right. Keep the words client and server, but do not dwell on formalism yet.

title: Afternoon Session
section: uv keeps the environment calm
text:
uv keeps the environment calm


uv lock
uv sync
uv run --directory .\mcp-servers\hello-world hello-world-server


Why it helps

fewer setup surprises
repeatable commands
good live-demo rhythm




Tell the room they do not need to memorize many commands. The repetition is part of the teaching design.

title: Afternoon Session
section: MCP concepts should stay concrete
text:
MCP concepts should stay concrete



Tool
Call a function



Resource
Read useful data



Prompt
Reuse task framing



Make it explicit that the class will mostly build tools. That keeps the scope manageable and visible.

title: Afternoon Session
section: A tiny server makes the pattern visible
text:
A tiny server makes the pattern visible
from mcp.server.fastmcp import FastMCP

mcp = FastMCP("hello-world")

@mcp.tool()
def ping(name: str) -> str:
    """Return a simple greeting."""
    return f"Hello, {name}!"

Pause here and ask the room which lines are ordinary Python and which line changes the role of the function.

title: Afternoon Session
section: The decorator is the turning point

title: Afternoon Session
section: FastMCP removes protocol plumbing from the first lesson
text:
FastMCP removes protocol plumbing from the first lesson



It handles

schema generation
argument validation
protocol wiring




That lets us teach

naming
tool design
response shape




Emphasize that abstractions are helping us teach the right layer first, not hiding important concepts forever.

title: Afternoon Session
section: Two small tools are enough for a walkthrough
text:
Two small tools are enough for a walkthrough



















Lookup tool







Useful demo







Status or data tool
















Canonical shape


One simple lookup plus one live-data or status tool is enough to teach discovery, invocation, and deployment.




Keep the class away from over-engineering. The goal is a pattern the room can reuse, not a large application.

title: Afternoon Session
section: Local inspection should happen before hosting
text:
Local inspection should happen before hosting





Inspect for

tool names
descriptions
parameters
output shape




Say clearly that a bad local loop becomes a worse cloud loop. Validate discoverability before deployment.

title: Afternoon Session
section: Deployment should feel like packaging, not magic
text:
Deployment should feel like packaging, not magic























Local project







Container image







Container Apps







Remote endpoint











The deployment story should stay linear. If the room loses the story here, the Azure steps will feel random.

title: Afternoon Session
section: Azure needs one clean visual anchor
text:
Azure needs one clean visual anchor


Replace this placeholder with your own Azure screenshot if you have one. Use it to keep the deployment explanation concrete.

title: Afternoon Session
section: Connection back to Copilot Studio closes the loop
text:
Connection back to Copilot Studio closes the loop























Hosted server







Connection setup







Agent can call tools







User sees value
















Why this matters


This is the moment the protocol stops feeling abstract.




Frame this as the payoff slide. The room has now seen a full path from code to capability.

title: Afternoon Session
section: The final discussion should stay practical
text:
The final discussion should stay practical



Internal lookups
What should become a tool



Public data blends
What outside signal helps



Governance
What still needs approval



End by translating the demo into attendee context. That is where future adoption ideas usually appear.

title: Afternoon Session
section: The takeaway is that MCP is approachable
text:
The takeaway is that MCP is approachable






Final message


The goal is not protocol expertise. The goal is confidence that this can be built, tested, and connected.




Close with confidence and restraint. The room should leave feeling that the path is understandable and repeatable.
